Contributing
====================

Thank you for your interest in contributing to FDSH! Your help makes this project better for everyone.

Before you start, please read our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

Ways you can contribute:
- Fix bugs in the code
- Improve documentation or fix typos
- Suggest or implement new features
- Improve the build process or tooling

Getting started:
1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Clone your fork locally:
   `git clone https://github.com/your-username/fdsh.git`
3. Create a new branch for your changes:
   `git checkout -b my-feature-branch`
4. Make your changes and commit them with clear messages:
   `git commit -m "Add feature X"` or `git commit -m "Fix typo in README"`
   You can add an optional extended description if you want, with an extra -m tag.
5. Push your branch to your fork:
   `git push origin my-feature-branch`
6. Open a Pull Request from your branch to the main FDSH repository

Coding style:
- Rust code should follow standard Rust formatting (rustfmt)
- Keep commits small and focused
- Write clear commit messages describing your changes

Pull Request review:
- PRs will be reviewed by the maintainers
- Be ready to discuss or revise your changes if needed
- Don’t worry if it takes some time; maintainers will get back to you

Other tips:
- Check existing issues before opening a new one
- Provide examples or screenshots when appropriate
- Ask questions in PR comments if something isn’t clear

Thank you again for contributing! Even small fixes and documentation improvements are hugely valuable, since, of course, I'm not a very good programmer myself.
